Package com.rhlowery.acs.service.impl

Class DefaultCatalogService

java.lang.Object
com.rhlowery.acs.service.impl.DefaultCatalogService
All Implemented Interfaces:
CatalogService
@ApplicationScoped public class DefaultCatalogService extends Object implements CatalogService
Default implementation of the CatalogService. Orchestrates metadata discovery and policy application across multiple CatalogProvider implementations. 
 [PlantUML: Provider registration and orchestration sequence]

    • DefaultCatalogService

      public DefaultCatalogService()

  • Method Details

    • registerProvider

      public void registerProvider(CatalogProvider provider)
      Manually registers a new catalog provider.
      Specified by:
      registerProvider in interface CatalogService
      Parameters:
      provider - The provider implementation to register

    • applyPolicy

      public void applyPolicy(String catalogId, String path, String action, String principal)
      Applies an access policy to a specific resource path in a catalog.
      Specified by:
      applyPolicy in interface CatalogService
      Parameters:
      catalogId - The ID of the target catalog
      path - The resource path (e.g. catalog.schema.table)
      action - The action to permit (e.g. SELECT, UPDATE)
      principal - The user or group receiving the permission
      Throws:
      RuntimeException - if the provider for catalogId is not found

    • getEffectivePermissions

      public String getEffectivePermissions(String catalogId, String path, String principal)
      Retrieves the effective permissions for a user on a specific resource.
      Specified by:
      getEffectivePermissions in interface CatalogService
      Parameters:
      catalogId - The ID of the target catalog
      path - The resource path
      principal - The user identifier
      Returns:
      The permission string (e.g. READ, WRITE, NONE)
      Throws:
      RuntimeException - if the provider for catalogId is not found

    • listProviders

      public List<String> listProviders()
      Lists all registered catalog providers with their implementation details.
      Specified by:
      listProviders in interface CatalogService
      Returns:
      A list of descriptive strings for each provider

    • getProviderRegistrations

      public List<CatalogRegistration> getProviderRegistrations()
      Retrieves structured registration metadata for all providers.
      Specified by:
      getProviderRegistrations in interface CatalogService
      Returns:
      A list of CatalogRegistration DTOs

    • getNodes

      public List<CatalogNode> getNodes(String catalogId, String path)
      Discovers child nodes (e.g. schemas, tables) for a given path.
      Specified by:
      getNodes in interface CatalogService
      Parameters:
      catalogId - The ID of the target catalog
      path - The parent path (empty string for root)
      Returns:
      A list of CatalogNode objects
      Throws:
      RuntimeException - if the provider for catalogId is not found

    • verifyPolicy

      public boolean verifyPolicy(String catalogId, String path, String expectedAction, String principal)
      Verifies if an access policy is correctly applied in the target system. Detects drift between ACS state and actual catalog state.
      Specified by:
      verifyPolicy in interface CatalogService
      Parameters:
      catalogId - The ID of the target catalog
      path - The resource path
      expectedAction - The action that should be permitted
      principal - The user identifier
      Returns:
      true if the system reflects the expected policy, false otherwise

    • getRequiredApprovers

      public List<String> getRequiredApprovers(String catalogId, String path)
      Identifies the required approvers for a specific resource path. Traverses up the hierarchy to find inherited approvers if not defined locally.
      Specified by:
      getRequiredApprovers in interface CatalogService
      Parameters:
      catalogId - The ID of the target catalog
      path - The resource path
      Returns:
      A list of approver user/group IDs

    • clear

      public void clear()
      Description copied from interface: CatalogService
      Resets all provider states (primarily for testing).
      Specified by:
      clear in interface CatalogService
